page_data.title: Listen and Respond
page_data.learning_objective: Listen to a peer and respond kindly.

activity.instruction: Complete one listening-and-choose task for “Listen and Respond”. Say one answer using a picture-supported phrase.

teacher_prompt.facilitation: Read or say the clue once, then repeat if needed.
questions: What can you show or tell about listen and respond?

parent_prompt.conversation: Repeat learning at home using everyday situations.
parent_prompt.home_activity: Repeat learning at home using everyday situations.

illustration.focal_point: One dominant focus for “Listen and Respond”.
illustration.style: Premium BCube preschool workbook illustration; clean white base; soft pastels; thick rounded outlines.
negative_constraints: No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
No more than six options or ambiguous answers.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-LKG-V3-P038

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-LKG-V3-P038
- Version: 3.0.0
- Book: Communication Champions
- Level: LKG (4+)
- Module: Module 5 – Stories and Expression
- Page: 38 of 41
- Exact title: Listen and Respond
- Source: Communication_Champions_LKG_Gold_Production_v2_Final.xlsx
- Source SHA-256: f9b5c4bbf026f68f6f46a9975b0407ca33b3e2351d2ecb92dcaa85518d7c97a3

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait page for “Listen and Respond”. Follow this deterministic package only. Produce no explanation, alternate, mockup, or additional page.

## 3. Engine order and precedence

1. Publishing Engine
2. Design Engine
3. Visual Grammar Engine
4. Educational Engine
5. Teaching Engine
6. Parent Partnership Engine
7. Illustration Engine
8. Character Engine
9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Precedence: approved page instruction > approved book/level rule > global rule. Do not resolve conflicts by guessing.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm; 3 mm bleed; minimum 10 mm safe margin; 12 mm binding margin; 300 DPI; CMYK-safe; print-ready.
- Use a 20–28 mm header with the exact title “Listen and Respond” centred and the official BCube logo reserved at top left.
- Use a 10–14 mm footer with page number 38 only at bottom right.
- Never generate or redraw the logo; apply the official vector asset during final layout.

## 5. Design and Visual Grammar Engines

- Page type: activity_page. Layout anchor: Choose a response.
- Visual/text balance: 60–70% visual and 30–40% text/activity. Use one dominant focal point and a clear title → model → action → response eye path.
- Blank-space rule: Use answer circles only. Maximum two core activities and one optional extension.
- Clean white base, soft BCube pastel accents, thick rounded outlines, large targets, strong contrast, generous spacing, and no visual clutter.

## 6. Educational Engine

- Objective: Listen to a peer and respond kindly.
- Primary activity: Complete one listening-and-choose task for “Listen and Respond”.
- Secondary activity: Say one answer using a picture-supported phrase.
- Observable evidence: I choose ______.
- Developmental response modes: oral response, guided tracing, short-word copying, sequencing, drawing, matching, or one supported sentence frame.

## 7. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Read or say the clue once, then repeat if needed.
- Model once, invite participation, pause for processing, scaffold through gesture/choice/model language, and affirm effort without shame.
- Teacher–student interaction must be visible, purposeful, inclusive, and connected to the exact learning objective.
- Assessment evidence: Child independently demonstrates mastery and communicates learning with confidence.

## 8. Parent Partnership Engine

- Home connection: Repeat learning at home using everyday situations.
- Use common household materials only; do not create a new scored task or require a device, purchase, or printing.

## 9. Illustration and Character Engines

- Composition: Follow the individual blueprint exactly.
- Show natural expressions, correct anatomy, inclusive children and families, clear turn-taking, unobstructed activity areas, and editable visible text.
- Star uses the locked bright-yellow rounded five-point identity, expressive face, blue shoes, and small blue cape; Star demonstrates or encourages without completing the child’s answer.

## 10. Approved source instruction

TECHNICAL AND PUBLISHING SPECIFICATION: Create an A4 portrait educational workbook page, 210 × 297 mm, 300 DPI, print-ready, with a clean white background, pastel accents, thick rounded black outlines, generous safe margins, and a premium preschool publishing finish. Use the official BCube logo at top left, exact page title centred at the top, and page number at bottom right. Do not show the LKG (4+) badge on interior pages. LKG DEVELOPMENT RULES: Designed for children aged approximately 4–5. Use familiar vocabulary, short model sentences, picture-supported instructions, guided tracing or copying, and one clear communication focus. Use a maximum of two core activities and one optional extension. TEACHING CLARITY RULES: Every page must show the exact child action, a short teacher or parent prompt, the expected child response, and purposeful response space. Do not use decorative blank balloons or unexplained boxes. Every visible frame, line, card, and balloon must have a learning purpose. PAGE-SPECIFIC ART DIRECTION: Exact title “Listen and Respond”, page number 38. Show one child sharing and another listening. Response cards: “That is interesting.”, “I like your idea.”, “Can you tell me more?” INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT: Primary activity: Complete one listening-and-choose task for “Listen and Respond”. Secondary task: Say one answer using a picture-supported phrase. Teacher prompt: Read or say the clue once, then repeat if needed. Expected child response: I choose ______. Blank-space allocation: Use answer circles only. Activity load: 2 activities. Do not add: No more than six options or ambiguous answers. OFFICIAL STAR MASCOT: Use one consistent bright yellow five-point Star with softly rounded points, large expressive eyes, rosy cheeks, friendly smile, blue shoes, and a small blue cape. Keep Star outside all response areas. QUALITY RULES: No watermark, unrelated logo, photorealism, gibberish text, distorted anatomy, extra fingers, crowded layouts, long passages, unsafe scenes, or blocked activity spaces. Use British English consistently.

## 11. Negative constraints

- No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
- No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
- No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
- No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
- No more than six options or ambiguous answers.

## 12. Gold QA gate and compiler footer

Verify page identity, geometry, bleed, safe zones, logo reservation, exact title, objective alignment, activity load, teacher interaction, parent extension, expected response, character continuity, inclusion, print suitability, prohibited extras, and unresolved variables. Any critical defect blocks release.

CC-LKG-V3-P038 | v3.0.0 | page 38/41 | Gold 100/100 | critical defects 0 | GOLD CERTIFIED.
